name: Classic Jollof Rice
vibe: The ultimate Nigerian crowd-pleaser. Perfect when you want something that feels like home.
steps: Blend tomatoes (or tomato paste with a little water), peppers, and half the onions into a smooth mix.
Heat oil in a pot. Fry remaining onions, then add the blend. Cook until thick and red.
Stir in washed rice, thyme, bay leaves, and seasoning cubes. Add stock or water to cover.
Cover and cook on medium-low 30–40 mins, stirring occasionally, until rice is tender.
Rest 5 minutes off heat. Serve hot; add chicken or other protein on the side if you have it.

name: Egusi Soup + Pounded Yam

measurements.pounded yam: 1 pack flour, or fresh yam for 5 people (or garri/fufu)

vibe: Hearty and satisfying. Best for when the family is around and you have a bit more time.
steps: Season and boil beef until tender. Reserve stock.
Blend peppers and onions. Fry palm oil, add blend, and cook down.
Stir in ground egusi. Cook until it thickens and oil floats.
Add stock, stockfish, crayfish, seasoning, and meat. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add spinach, simmer 5 more minutes.
Prepare swallow: pound yam, or make eba from garri, or prepare fufu. Serve soup with the swallow.

name: Fish Pepper Soup

measurements.fish: 1–1.2 kg whole or steaks (catfish, tilapia, or any firm fish)

vibe: Quick, warming, and comforting. Perfect when someone is not feeling too well or it's a cold evening.
steps: Clean fish and cut into chunks. Season lightly with salt and seasoning cube.
Boil water with blended peppers, onions, ginger, and garlic.
Add fish and crayfish. Cook gently 12–15 mins — don't stir too much.
Add scent leaves and utazi. Simmer 3 minutes and serve hot.

name: Beans & Plantain (Ewa Dodo)
vibe: Budget-friendly, filling, and loved by both children and adults. Great weeknight option.
steps: Pick stones from beans. Boil with onions until very soft.
Mash or stir beans to a thick porridge. Season with salt, crayfish, and seasoning.
Fry pepper sauce in palm oil until rich and red. Stir into beans or serve on top.
Slice and fry ripe plantain until golden (the dodo).
Serve beans with fried plantain on the side.

name: Okra Soup with Fufu or Garri

measurements.garri: 2–3 cups for eba (or use fufu / pounded yam)

vibe: Light but satisfying. Good when you want something that won't feel too heavy.
steps: Boil beef with seasoning until tender. Set stock aside.
Blend okra lightly (or chop finely). Fry pepper-onion mix in palm oil.
Add okra and stock. Stir until draw starts forming.
Add meat, stockfish, crayfish, and spinach. Simmer 10 minutes.
Make eba from garri, or prepare fufu/pounded yam. Serve soup with the swallow.

name: Suya-Spiced Grilled Chicken + Salad
vibe: Fast and full of flavour. Good when you want something different from the usual soups.
steps: Cut chicken into bite-size pieces. Coat with suya spice and a little oil.
Grill or pan-sear on high heat until charred and cooked through.
Toss sliced tomatoes, cabbage, onions, and peppers for a fresh salad.
Serve hot suya chicken over the salad.

name: Party Fried Rice
vibe: Colourful and festive. When you want the house to smell like a proper owambe.
steps: Parboil rice, drain, and spread to cool slightly.
Stir-fry diced chicken (or beef/liver) in hot oil until golden. Set aside.
Fry onions and peppers. Add carrots, green beans, curry powder, and seasoning cubes.
Toss in rice. Stir-fry on high heat until evenly coloured and heated through.
Return protein, mix once more, and serve immediately.

name: Yam Porridge (Asaro)
vibe: Soft, saucy, and deeply comforting. Your grandma would approve of this one.
steps: Peel and cube yam (or sweet potato). Rinse and set aside.
Fry pepper-tomato-onion mix in palm oil until thick. Add seasoning.
Add yam and enough water to cover. Cook until yam softens.
Mash lightly in the pot for a porridge texture.
Add crayfish, stockfish, and spinach. Simmer 5 minutes and serve.

name: Moi Moi

measurements.beans: 3 cups peeled honey beans (or 2 cups bean flour)

vibe: Soft, protein-packed, and perfect for lunchboxes or a light dinner.
steps: Peel beans (or use bean flour). Blend with peppers and onions until smooth.
Mix in oil, seasoning cubes, and crayfish. Adjust thickness with a little water.
Pour into moi moi wraps or bowls. Add egg slices if using.
Steam 45–55 minutes until firm and cooked through.
Cool slightly before unwrapping. Serve warm.

name: Tuwo Shinkafa + Miyan Taushe

measurements.groundnut oil: 1/2 cup (or groundnut paste cooked out with a little oil)

vibe: Proper Hausa comfort food. Sweet, nutty soup that feels special without being fussy.
steps: Boil soft rice with extra water until very soft. Mash into tuwo and set aside.
Blend peppers and onions. Cook groundnut paste or groundnut oil with the blend until fragrant and thick.
Add beef stock, seasoning cubes, and cooked beef. Simmer 20 minutes.
Stir in pumpkin leaves (or spinach). Cook 5 more minutes.
Serve miyan taushe with tuwo shinkafa (the mashed rice).

name: Akara & Pap (Quick Breakfast)

measurements.vegetable oil: for deep frying (about 2–3 cups in pan)
measurements.pap: 1–1½ cups dry ogi/akamu (or ready pap for 3 people)

vibe: Saturday morning energy. Crispy akara with warm pap — children finish theirs fast.
steps: PAP (ogi/akamu): Mix dry pap with a little cold water to a smooth paste. Stir into boiling water (or heat gently) until thick and glossy. Sweeten if you like. Keep warm — this is half the dish.
AKARA: Peel beans and blend with peppers and onions to a thick paste.
Whisk in salt and seasoning. The mix should hold its shape on a spoon.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil. Fry until deep golden on both sides.
Drain akara on paper. Serve hot with the warm pap (or soft bread as a substitute for pap).

name: Yam & Egg Sauce
vibe: Weeknight classic. Soft yam with rich tomato-egg sauce — filling without fuss.
steps: Peel and slice yam. Boil in salted water until just tender. Drain and keep warm.
Blend or chop tomatoes, peppers, and onions.
Heat oil, fry the blend until thick and the raw taste is gone. Season with cubes and thyme.
Crack eggs into the sauce and scramble gently, or fry eggs separately and fold in.
Serve hot sauce over the boiled yam.

name: Nigerian Spaghetti
vibe: Crowded-house favourite. Colourful, saucy pasta that disappears from the pot.
steps: Boil spaghetti in salted water until al dente. Drain and toss with a little oil.
If using chicken, season and fry or boil until cooked. Set aside.
Fry onions in oil, add blended tomatoes and peppers. Cook until thick and oil floats.
Add curry, thyme, seasoning, and optional carrots/green beans. Simmer 5 minutes.
Fold in spaghetti (and chicken). Toss well and serve hot.

name: Coconut Rice

measurements.coconut milk: 2 cups (or 1 tin + water to make 2 cups)

vibe: Creamy, fragrant rice with a gentle sweetness. Feels special on a Sunday.
steps: Rinse rice. Optionally parboil and drain.
Fry onions (and a little pepper/tomato if using) in oil until soft.
Pour in coconut milk, seasoning, curry, and thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add rice, stir once, cover and cook on low until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Fluff with a fork. Serve with chicken or stew if you like.

name: Miyan Kuka + Tuwo
vibe: Northern comfort in a bowl. Earthy kuka soup with soft tuwo — proper home food.
steps: Boil beef with seasoning and ginger until tender. Reserve stock.
Make tuwo: cook soft rice with extra water, mash until smooth and firm. Keep warm.
In a pot, heat palm oil lightly. Add blended peppers and onions; cook briefly.
Pour in stock. Sprinkle kuka powder while stirring so it does not cake. Simmer until thick and drawy.
Add crayfish and meat. Adjust salt. Serve miyan kuka with tuwo (or garri/fufu).

name: Nigerian Tomato Stew with White Rice

measurements.tomatoes: 5–6 medium or 1 tin paste + fresh tomatoes

vibe: Everyday Nigerian plate: plain rice with deep red tomato stew. Search-friendly as stew, tomato stew, or rice and stew.
steps: Boil rice in salted water until soft and fluffy. Drain if needed and keep warm.
Season chicken with salt, curry, and thyme. Boil or fry until cooked. Reserve stock if boiled.
Blend tomatoes, peppers, onions, ginger, and garlic.
Heat oil, fry the blend until thick, dark red, and oil rises. Season with cubes.
Add chicken and a splash of stock. Simmer 10 minutes. Serve stew over white rice.
